codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed FAIL verdict and identified additional issues beyond the original AC4 shell-quoting finding: (1) AC4 check uses wrong JSON schema path d[agents][ba] instead of d[global][agents][ba]; (2) ; echo 0 suffix in AC1/AC2 check commands masks exit codes making exit_code:1 expectations permanently unfalsifiable; (3) residual 12+ drift in context success_criteria[0] not updated when R2 was corrected; (4) arch-4 inconsistency broader than ticket line 26 — also at line 62. AC4 delta 108 confirmed correct (ba close_success_qa_pass = 8).
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex findings 1-4 and 6 incorporated as blocking objections OBJ-1 through OBJ-4. Findings 5/7/8/9/10 classified as OBSERVATION_ONLY.

summary: BA resolved all 7 prior blocking objections from iteration 1. However, 4 new blocking defects found in iteration 2: (1) AC4 check command is shell-broken (unescaped inner double-quotes cause SyntaxError); (2) AC4 check uses wrong JSON schema path d[agents][ba] instead of d[global][agents][ba]; (3) ; echo 0 suffix in AC1/AC2 check.commands makes exit_code:1 expectations permanently unfalsifiable; (4) residual 12+ drift in context success_criteria[0] contradicts the corrected exactly-12 in R2 and AC1. FAIL.

objections.claim: AC4 check command in acceptance-criteria-20260522-000000.json is executable
objections.problem: AC4 check.commands[0] uses unescaped inner double-quotes inside outer double-quotes. Shell splits the -c argument at first inner quote; Python receives: open(/tmp/test-scores.json) without string delimiters and d[agents][ba][history] with bare identifiers, producing SyntaxError: invalid syntax. VERIFIED: bash eval of exact stored command returns exit code 1 with SyntaxError.
objections.required_fix: Wrap python -c argument in single quotes: python3 -c 'import json; d=json.load(open("/tmp/test-scores.json")); h=d["global"]["agents"]["ba"]["history"][-1]; print(h.get("uncapped_delta"), h.get("new_score"))'  (also incorporates OBJ-2 schema path fix)
objections.evidence: bash eval of stored command: SyntaxError: invalid syntax, exit code 1. Verified 2026-05-22.

objections.claim: AC4 check command accesses history via correct schema path d[agents][ba][history][-1]
objections.problem: score-update.sh:57 initializes the file as {"global":{"agents":{}}, ...} and score-update.sh:146-147 accesses data["global"]["agents"]. The correct path is d["global"]["agents"]["ba"]["history"][-1]. The AC4 check uses d["agents"]["ba"] which is the wrong top-level key. If dev creates a properly-structured mock file matching the real schema and runs score-update.sh, the check command will raise KeyError.

objections.claim: AC1 and AC2 check commands can verify exit_code: 1 from git check-ignore
objections.problem: AC1 check.commands[0]: git check-ignore -q docs/reference/tmp-cleanup-convention.md; echo 0 with expected exit_code: 1. The ; echo 0 suffix unconditionally produces exit code 0 (from echo), overriding any exit code from git check-ignore. exit_code: 1 expectation is permanently unfalsifiable regardless of gitignore state. AC2 check.commands[0] has the identical ; echo 0 pattern with exit_code: 1 expected.
objections.required_fix: Remove ; echo 0 from commands where exit_code: 1 is expected. Or use explicit capture: git check-ignore -q <path>; rc=$?; test "$rc" -eq 1
objections.evidence: acceptance-criteria-20260522-000000.json: AC1 cmd ends with ; echo 0, expected exit_code: 1. AC2 first cmd same. echo always returns 0.

objections.claim: context success_criteria[0] is consistent with R2 and AC1
objections.problem: context requirement.success_criteria[0] says git ls-files docs/reference/ returns 12+ files. R2 acceptance_criterion says exactly 12. AC1 then says wc -l returns exactly 12. Three locations in the same context JSON / AC file, two different semantics. The success_criteria array was not updated when R2 was corrected in iteration 2.
objections.required_fix: Update context requirement.success_criteria[0] to: git ls-files docs/reference/ | wc -l returns exactly 12
objections.evidence: context success_criteria[0]: 12+ files. requirements_decomposition[R2]: exactly 12. AC1.then: exactly 12. Divergent semantics in same artifact.

observations_only.observation: AC1/AC2 git check-ignore can false-pass after git add -f because check-ignore exits 1 for tracked files. Using --no-index tests the rule itself independently.
